summary: iter150 — full-stack testing of (1) profile photo gallery, (2) Nano Banana AI filters, (3) referral anti-fraud. 12/12 backend pytest tests PASSED including a successful real Nano Banana generation. Frontend: PhotoGallery mounts with 'Vintage' badge; MediaFilterEditor renders CSS + 5 AI presets; clicking cartoon shows loading overlay, generation completes and overlay disappears with no error.
critical_code_review_comments: ai_filters.py: `used2, cap2 = used + 1, cap` — the used+1 header is computed from the pre-insert COUNT; since the pending row is inserted before the call but only success rows are counted, the user's real 'used' after success is used+1 only if the COUNT was taken before INSERT succeeded. This is fine but could be made explicit via a re-COUNT to avoid off-by-one under concurrency.
ai_filters.py: the `idx_user_id` mentioned in the docstring is actually `idx_ai_filter_user_recent` — update comment to match DDL.

users.py photo_gallery: joins `follows` table with columns (follower_id, followed_id). Verify this is the right table name (some routes use `user_follows`). Mixed table naming was seen in routes/users.py (`user_follows` at line 58 vs `follows` at line 357). This could cause private-account gallery privacy check to fail silently.
referral_fraud_service.fraud_report uses f-string interpolation for the days value inside SQL — days is clamped (1..90) so safe, but prefer parameter binding for consistency.
No rate-limit on POST /api/media/ai-filter at the HTTP level beyond the daily cap — a user could spam concurrent requests before any row is counted. Minor, but worth a per-minute cap.
test_credentials: Bob bob@japap.com/Test1234!, Admin admin@japap.com/JapapAdmin2024!, captcha bypass JAPAP_E2E_BYPASS_2026
seed_data_creation: Two stories inserted for Bob during tests (image_url + filter_preset=vintage / invalid variants). No cleanup — test prefixes are unique URL uuids.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run pytest with PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1 because web3's pytest_ethereum plugin breaks collection due to an eth_typing version mismatch (cannot import ContractName). Public URL can be slow — use 60s timeouts on login. For frontend automation, login via page.evaluate(fetch(/api/auth/login)) to bypass Cloudflare bot protection, then stash access_token in localStorage under keys japap_token/access_token/token plus japap_user/user. Nano Banana generation is working (took ~30s on pro-business Bob).
